Vote: Against 
Gauntlet votes against this proposal given several factors, including the current transition toward OpCo, the lack of specification around incentive optimization and strategies, and too broad of a mandate regarding the type of dApps targeted. 
  TMC Stablecoin Recommendation  (Snapshot) 
Vote: Abstain 
As an applicant, Gauntlet has abstained from this proposal. 
  TMC ARB Recommendation  (Snapshot) 
Vote: Abstain 
As an applicant, Gauntlet has abstained from this proposal. 
  OpCo – Oversight and Transparency Committee (OAT) Elections  (Snapshot) 
Vote: A.J. Warner, Patrick McCorry, Frisson 
We have voted to elect A.J., Patrick, and Frisson for the OAT. There were a number of strong candidates up for election, but ultimately, our decisions stemmed from our historical understanding and interpretation of the need for OpCo to function as a mesh layer between OCL, Arbitrum DAO, and the Arbitrum Foundation. As OpCo evolves, we expect this board to change. 
 Still, at the initial structure, we think these parties represent able and knowledgeable representatives who are best positioned to guide the structure of OpCo moving forward. 
 In particular, Frisson and Patrick are likely the two most knowledgeable applicants of the existing operational structure required to interface between the Arbitrum DAO and its legal wrapper, the Arbitrum Foundation. These are exactly the experts needed to ensure the structure of OpCo is efficient and accountable to DAO stakeholders. 
  [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Audit Program  (Onchain) 
Vote: For 
Gauntlet has voted in favor of the Arbitrum Audit Program and believe it represents an opportunity for the Arbitrum Foundation to demonstrate it’s operational capacity to operate grant programs on behalf of the Arbitrum DAO."},"0xdd38e103e337b9e02a9d9ac73c2587db5be6cfb46506e13c0e897f7331895cf4":{"validRationale":false,"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/21156/45","voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/tmc-proposed-allocations-v2-stablecoin-strategy/28786","createdAt":"2025-04-02T19:28:39.000Z","rationale":"[Non-constitutional] ARB Incentives: User Acquisition for dApps & Protocols  (Snapshot)

Vote: For 
Gauntlet has voted in favor of this proposal to activate treasury management of DAO ETH by supporting it’s use in DeFi. Aave, Fluid, and Camelot represent strong DeFi protocols on Arbitrum, and earning yield on DAO-held treasury while stimulating liquidity in the Arbitrum ecosystem is a meaningful use of DAO treasury funds. That said, there are still questions regarding the setup of the council with meaningful conflict of interest considerations, however these are considerations for the future rather than reasons to block a reasonable program today. 
 We also would like greater sophistication around the determined deposit amount, particularly POL. If the DAO allocates liquidity to a protocol, it should have a thesis and expected KPIs for this liquidity’s effect in stimulating trading volume (AMM) or borrowing activity (Lending). 
 Moving forward, we expect OpCo to take a more neutral stance in these programs and establish more sophisticated entities or protocols capable of managing active liquidity beyond DAO voting. 
  [CONSTITUTIONAL] Proposal: For Arbitrum DAO to register the Sky Custom Gateway contracts in the Router  (Snapshot) 
Vote: For 
Gauntlet has voted in favor of this proposal to support USDS and sUSDS via the Router contracts. We believe this change will enable a better UI for Spark bridgers and users. 
  [CONSTITUTIONAL] - Adopt Timeboost + Nova Fee Sweep  (Onchain) 
Vote: For 
Gauntlet has voted in favor of Timeboost and the Nova Fee Sweep. While we strongly support the Nova Fee Sweep, Gauntlet trusts Offchain Labs to endorse Timeboost. Gauntlet has some hesitations regarding the mechanisms’ implications for certain onchain activities. That said, given the core team’s belief in the technology and its benefits, we will support the ecosystem in the experiment of launching Timeboost onchain. 
  [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Onboarding V2: A Governance Bootcamp  
Vote: Abstain 
This was a difficult proposal, which the Arbitrum Foundation eventually flagged for its expense budget despite no immediate solution being provided. While we understand those concerns, the program is also the only program currently available to serve this need, and we do not believe that it is inherently a waste of funds if overpriced. 
 We believe this proposal was signaled out, perhaps unfairly, compared to other initiatives. If meaningful participants are attracted, $17K per recruited participant is less than the DAO has paid a council member to check boxes and provide low-to-negligent service for some council positions. It’s also not outrageous given the proposed engagement these participants would have compared to the “acquisition costs” of events. 
 Gauntlet believes DAOs remain an important avenue for emergent talent, and Arbitrum is no exception. An Abstain vote here represents our conviction that this program should be re-evaluated and re-designed with 404 DAO and the Foundation to find an honest compromise before returning to vote or re-structured within OpCo."},"# [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Audit Pr":{"validRationale":false,"voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":null,"createdAt":"2025-04-02T19:27:26.000Z","post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/21156/43","reason":null,"rationale":"GMC’s Preferred Allocations (7,500 ETH)  (Snapshot)

{"score":"0.00","tn":5,"rn":0,"breakdown":{"0x9d13c56f911796a578812959f775092b3644901be17738a2d41c516d8a8f2e9b":{"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/gfx-labs-delegate-communication-thread/13794","voted":true,"createdAt":"2025-04-03T13:31:58.000Z"},"0xdd38e103e337b9e02a9d9ac73c2587db5be6cfb46506e13c0e897f7331895cf4":{"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/gfx-labs-delegate-communication-thread/13794","voted":true,"createdAt":"2025-04-03T13:33:01.000Z"},"0xb422848b0f53f9077444b922d553618e331f9abe7539ae4566ff84364d0fbd3c":{"validRationale":false,"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/13794/82","voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/opco-oversight-and-transparency-committee-oat-elections/28867","createdAt":"2025-04-03T13:34:23.000Z","rationale":"4 Polls Closing April 3, 2025  
  TMC ARB Recommendation 
  Summary:  This poll asks ARB holders if they support deploying 10,000,000 ARB into an onchain strategy with Karpatkey and Avantgarde. 
  NB: The Treasury Management Committee recommends no allocation.  
  Recommendation: Vote NO, Deploy Nothing.  The Treasury Management Committee recommends no allocation based on risks, reward, and complexity in monitoring. 
  TMC Stablecoin Recommendation 
  Summary:  This poll asks ARB holders if they support liquidating 15,000,000 ARB and then deploying the stablecoins into onchain strategies with Karpatkey, Avantgarde, and Gauntlet. 
  Recommendation: Vote NO, Deploy Nothing.  We wish these three recommended strategies could be voted on separately, because they are not especially similar in risk, return, operational complexity, or fee structure. In particular, Gauntlet is offering a simple strategy with no fees charged to governance, while the others have significant need of active management, uncertain returns projected, and come with AUM fees that may or may not be justified. 
  OpCo – Oversight and Transparency Committee (OAT) Elections 
  Summary:  This poll asks ARB holders which three candidates they support for the Oversight and Transparency Committee for the newly authorized OpCo. Two additional members will be appointed by the three elected members. 
 Candidates: 
A.J. Warner (ajwarner90) 
Belmin Kalkan (0xRecruiter) 
Chris Cameron (PaperImperium) 
Edgar Prediger (EzR3aL) 
Federico Daffina (Federico) 
Frisson (Frisson) 
Jana Bertram (Janabe) 
Joseph Schiarizzi (cupojoseph) 
Marc Zeller (MarcZeller) 
Patrick McCorry (stonecoldpat) 
Paul Imseih (Pablo) 
Pedro Breuer (pedrob) 
  Recommendation: Vote 33% Chris Cameron (PaperImperium), 33% Joseph Schiarizzi (cupojoseph), 33% Pedro Bruer (pedrob), 1% Frisson (Frisson).  Joseph, Pedro, and Frisson all are thoughtful with attention to detail, and a commitment to transparency. 
  NB: This vote distribution complies with the code of conduct covering self-voting.  
  [Non-constitutional] ARB Incentives: User Acquisition for dApps & Protocols 
  Summary:  This poll asks ARB holders if they wish to authorize a $3,000,000 budget for off-chain advertising campaigns to attract users to Arbitrum. The focus primarily is on bridges/wallets and DeFi. 
 We encourage voters to carefully review  the full proposal here , with full details on execution, strategy, and justification. 
  Recommendation: Vote Against.  We would support this with a smaller initial budget, with a larger follow-on budget if results materialize. Overall, we agree this is a blind spot for Arbitrum’s ecosystem, and with experimenting with at a lower total budget."},"0x7689f957c8cc37e991c7bed0f1d3dd3f6024a921b0bfbae5c897a09ab45c538b":{"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/gfx-labs-delegate-communication-thread/13794","voted":true,"createdAt":"2025-04-03T13:35:29.000Z"},"# [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Audit Pr":{"validRationale":false,"voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":null,"createdAt":"2025-03-27T17:29:00.000Z","post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/13794/80","reason":"Vote Against. These programs are industry standard, but this is several multiples the size of the comparable program at Optimism, which has around $1.2m for the first half of 2025. We would fully support this program with a smaller size.","rationale":"2 Polls Closing March 27, 2025  
  [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Onboarding V2: A Governance Bootcamp 
  Summary:  This poll asks ARB holders if they support providing ~$249,000 to renew the Onboarding Working Group to recruit and train new governance participants. 
  Recommendation: Vote Against.  As we stated at the Snapshot vote in late December, we support the concept, but are not clear what was accomplished with the first iteration of this program. Additionally, Arbitrum does not appear to suffer from “not enough hands”, so a generalized recruitment for generic governance participants probably isn’t worth the time and resources, which we would prefer to see spent on sourcing more delegators or specialized contributors that Arbitrum may not have access to through governance or hiring. 
  [CONSTITUTIONAL] - Adopt Timeboost + Nova Fee Sweep 
  Summary:  This poll asks ARB holders to approve sweeping the remaining fees from Arbitrum Nova to the treasury, as well as activating Timeboost which enables priority pricing.  Technical details for Timeboost can be found in this repo.  
  Recommendation: Vote For.  
 Fee sweep: Newly accruing fees are automatically swept now through the recently deployed router. But historically accrued fees still require this manual transfer to the treasury.  Fees accumulated can be viewed at the address here . This process should not require repeating with the new router in place. 
 Timeboost: Arbitrum’s fee structure has yielded poor returns historically, particularly compared to rivals like Optimism. The main difference that is driving the divergence in returns is the lack of a priority fee on Arbitrum. Timeboost should be a major increase in revenue. 
 Generally, we are not in favor of bundling unrelated items into a single vote, and would recommend this be two separate votes in the future.","penalty":0}}}

{"score":"0.00","tn":5,"rn":0,"breakdown":{"0x9d13c56f911796a578812959f775092b3644901be17738a2d41c516d8a8f2e9b":{"validRationale":false,"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/13942/22","voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/tmc-proposed-allocations-v2-arb-strategy/28785","createdAt":"2025-04-01T11:13:30.000Z","rationale":"Proposal:  The Watchdog: Arbitrum DAO’s Grant Misuse Bounty Program  
  Vote : For 
  Rationale : Establishing The Watchdog program strengthens the DAO’s integrity by creating incentives for the community to surface fund misappropriation, which has historically gone unchecked. This initiative fosters accountability, helps deter malicious behavior, and ultimately protects DAO capital. 
   Proposal:  Arbitrum D.A.O. Season 3 Elections - Gaming  
  Vote : 100% for Floock 
  Rationale : We gave 100% of our voting weight to Flook in the Gaming domain because their track record demonstrates an ability to identify high-potential projects and allocate grants in a way that maximizes impact. 
   Proposal:  Arbitrum D.A.O. Season 3 Elections - Dev Tooling on One and Stylus  
  Vote : 100% for Juandi 
  Rationale : We gave 100% of our voting weight to Juandi due to their strong technical background, clear alignment with the DAO’s priorities, and hands-on experience with Arbitrum’s developer ecosystem. 
   Proposal:  Arbitrum D.A.O. Season 3 Elections - Education, Community Growth, and Events  
  Vote : 100% for SEEDGov 
  Rationale : We allocated 100% of our voting power to SEEDGov based on their consistent contributions to governance education, community onboarding, and their thoughtful approach to ecosystem engagement. 
   Proposal:  Arbitrum D.A.O. Season 3 Elections - New Protocols and Ideas  
  Vote : 50% for Saurabh 50% for CastleCapital 
  Rationale : We split our vote evenly between Saurabh and CastleCapital as both candidates brought strong, complementary qualifications to the table. 
   Proposal:  [Constitutional AIP] Activate Arbitrum BoLD + Infura Nova Validator Whitelist  
  Vote : For 
  Rationale : Enabling BoLD is a major milestone toward decentralizing Arbitrum One through permissionless validation, while maintaining strong security guarantees. 
   Proposal:  Arbitrum Growth Circles Event Proposal  
  Vote : For 
  Rationale : We support this proposal because with an experienced team and clear KPIs, this initiative directly aligns with the DAO’s goals of ecosystem development and community-led growth. 
   Proposal:  Arbitrum D.A.O. (Domain Allocator Offerings) Grant Program - Season 3  
  Vote : For 
  Rationale : StableLab Supports the QB grants program in Arbitrum. The first 2 seasons have proven to be successful and we are excited to see what they fund in season 3. 
   Proposal:  TMC recommendation  
  Vote :  #1  Deploy Both Strategies Abstain  #4  Deploy Nothing  #2  Only Deploy ARB Strategy  #3  Only Deploy Stable Strategy 
  Rationale : We support this proposal and prioritize deploying both the Stablecoin and ARB strategies ( #1 ). While we appreciate the TMC’s cautious approach, we believe deploying a portion of the DAO’s ARB holdings into on-chain strategies complements the stablecoin allocation and helps reduce idle asset exposure. 
   Proposal:  GMC’s Preferred Allocations (7,500 ETH)  
  Vote : For deploy capital 
  Rationale : We support this proposal as it strikes a strong balance between conservative yield generation and ecosystem growth by deploying 7,500 ETH across trusted protocols like Lido, Aave, Fluid, and Camelot. 
   Proposal:  [CONSTITUTIONAL] Proposal: For Arbitrum DAO to register the Sky Custom Gateway contracts in the Router  
  Vote : For 
  Rationale : We support this proposal because it improves the user experience and functionality of the Arbitrum Bridge. This ensures users can seamlessly bridge stablecoins via the official UI. The contracts are already deployed, audited, and live, making this a low-risk, high-impact integration for the Arbitrum ecosystem. 
   Proposal:  [CONSTITUTIONAL] - Adopt Timeboost + Nova Fee Sweep  
  Vote : For 
  Rationale : The Nove Fee Sweep Action efficiently consolidates 1,885 ETH in historical Nova fees into the Arbitrum DAO Treasury using modern, automated infrastructure. This action improves treasury transparency, reduces governance friction, and completes the transition to the upgraded fee routing system, strengthening the DAO’s financial operations with minimal risk. 
   Proposal:  [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Onboarding V2: A Governance Bootcamp  
  Vote : Against 
  Rationale : We voted against this proposal due to concerns around cost-efficiency, unclear long-term impact, and limited evidence of effectiveness from the initial pilot. 
   Proposal:  [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Audit Program  
  Vote : For 
  Rationale : This initiative not only enhances on-chain security and reduces risk across the Arbitrum ecosystem, but it also promotes responsible scaling, supports developer adoption, and encourages innovation on Arbitrum chains."},"0xdd38e103e337b9e02a9d9ac73c2587db5be6cfb46506e13c0e897f7331895cf4":{"validRationale":false,"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/13942/22","voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/tmc-proposed-allocations-v2-stablecoin-strategy/28786","createdAt":"2025-03-31T23:35:27.000Z","rationale":"Proposal:  The Watchdog: Arbitrum DAO’s Grant Misuse Bounty Program

  Vote : For 
  Rationale : Enabling BoLD is a major milestone toward decentralizing Arbitrum One through permissionless validation, while maintaining strong security guarantees. 
   Proposal:  Arbitrum Growth Circles Event Proposal  
  Vote : For 
  Rationale : We support this proposal because with an experienced team and clear KPIs, this initiative directly aligns with the DAO’s goals of ecosystem development and community-led growth. 
   Proposal:  Arbitrum D.A.O. (Domain Allocator Offerings) Grant Program - Season 3  
  Vote : For 
  Rationale : StableLab Supports the QB grants program in Arbitrum. The first 2 seasons have proven to be successful and we are excited to see what they fund in season 3. 
   Proposal:  TMC recommendation  
  Vote :  #1  Deploy Both Strategies Abstain  #4  Deploy Nothing  #2  Only Deploy ARB Strategy  #3  Only Deploy Stable Strategy 
  Rationale : We support this proposal and prioritize deploying both the Stablecoin and ARB strategies ( #1 ). While we appreciate the TMC’s cautious approach, we believe deploying a portion of the DAO’s ARB holdings into on-chain strategies complements the stablecoin allocation and helps reduce idle asset exposure. 
   Proposal:  GMC’s Preferred Allocations (7,500 ETH)  
  Vote : For deploy capital 
  Rationale : We support this proposal as it strikes a strong balance between conservative yield generation and ecosystem growth by deploying 7,500 ETH across trusted protocols like Lido, Aave, Fluid, and Camelot. 
   Proposal:  [CONSTITUTIONAL] Proposal: For Arbitrum DAO to register the Sky Custom Gateway contracts in the Router  
  Vote : For 
  Rationale : We support this proposal because it improves the user experience and functionality of the Arbitrum Bridge. This ensures users can seamlessly bridge stablecoins via the official UI. The contracts are already deployed, audited, and live, making this a low-risk, high-impact integration for the Arbitrum ecosystem. 
   Proposal:  [CONSTITUTIONAL] - Adopt Timeboost + Nova Fee Sweep  
  Vote : For 
  Rationale : The Nove Fee Sweep Action efficiently consolidates 1,885 ETH in historical Nova fees into the Arbitrum DAO Treasury using modern, automated infrastructure. This action improves treasury transparency, reduces governance friction, and completes the transition to the upgraded fee routing system, strengthening the DAO’s financial operations with minimal risk. 
   Proposal:  [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Onboarding V2: A Governance Bootcamp  
  Vote : Against 
  Rationale : We voted against this proposal due to concerns around cost-efficiency, unclear long-term impact, and limited evidence of effectiveness from the initial pilot. 
   Proposal:  [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Audit Program  
  Vote : For

{"score":"0.00","tn":5,"rn":0,"breakdown":{"0x9d13c56f911796a578812959f775092b3644901be17738a2d41c516d8a8f2e9b":{"validRationale":false,"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/26376/13","voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/tmc-proposed-allocations-v2-arb-strategy/28785","createdAt":"2025-04-02T16:19:47.000Z","rationale":"Proposal:  TMC Stablecoin Recommendation  
  Vote: FOR  
 Type: Off-chain  
 Rationale : We agree with the TMC’s recommendation on the proposed stablecoin strategies. We believe the potential returns are attractive, while mitigating risks. We will therefore be voting in support. 
 
   Proposal:  TMC ARB Recommendation  
  Vote: FOR  
 Type: Off-chain  
 Rationale : We expressed our rationale for voting in support of this proposal in the forum thread below: 
 
 
  
  TMC Proposed Allocations V2 -- ARB Strategy  
 
 We will be voting FOR the ARB strategy. 
 While we acknowledge the TMC’s reservations and respect the reasoning behind their recommendation, we find the information provided by  @Karpatkey  and  @Avantgarde  to be transparent and compelling (including the backtesting, historical data analysis, proposed execution mechanisms and risk mitigation strategies, etc). 
 The strategy offers strong downside protection by guaranteeing premium income even if ARB’s price declines further, while the primary tradeoff is a capped upside due to the strike price. Although avoiding conversion at the strike price would be ideal, it’s worth emphasizing that the DAO treasury already holds sufficient ARB reserves to capitalize on potential price appreciation. This ensures the broader treasury retains exposure to upside gains, even if the strategy itself limits immediate participation beyond the strike threshold. 
 
 
 
   Proposal:  OpCo – Oversight and Transparency Committee (OAT) Elections  
  Vote: Multi-choice  
 Type: Off-chain  
 Rationale:  
 Marc Zeller - 25%  
 Having been long-time delegates and contributors to Aave, we’re aware from a first-hand perspective how effective ACI’s leadership of the Aave DAO has been. They’ve ensured a streamlined DAO which has consolidated Aave’s position in the market and made it a profitable, lean, revenue-generating protocol with a clear focus. 
  Edgar Prediger (EzR3aL) - 25%  
 EzR3aL has also been an important part of Aave’s success and has engaged deeply with service providers and provided a lot of valuable feedback. We have engaged a lot with him over the years, and combined with his professional background from a non-crypto perspective, we think he would make a great candidate to oversee the DAO’s initiatives. 
  AJ Warner - 25%  
 As other delegates have mentioned, there is a need for a deep link between Offchain Labs and the DAO. AJ has significant context and responsibilities at OCL, and believe he will do a great job in facilitating the information flow and priorities between the OpCo and OCL. We also think it is important for the Foundation to have a seat at the table, and we did not vote for Pat because the AF already is an observing member. 
  Frisson - 25%  
 Frisson has deep context with the DAO, knows how it works well, and has a good view into the issues it faces especially with the MSS problems that were faced. It is impressive how Frisson has taken leadership of the MSS and seemingly turned it around. Frisson has significant leadership experience at Tally and a bird’s eye view of the ecosystem. As such, we believe he will play a key role in overseeing the OpCo."},"0xdd38e103e337b9e02a9d9ac73c2587db5be6cfb46506e13c0e897f7331895cf4":{"validRationale":false,"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/26376/13","voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/tmc-proposed-allocations-v2-stablecoin-strategy/28786","createdAt":"2025-04-02T16:23:10.000Z","rationale":"Proposal:  TMC Stablecoin Recommendation

  Vote :  FOR  
 Type :  On-Chain  
 Rationale : We voted in support of this proposal during the snapshot vote based on the rationale  here . 
 While several concerns have been raised regarding the program’s cost and retention of fellows, we nonetheless believe this proposal is a worthy experiment for the DAO to fund. During our  Governance Brunch at EthDenver  (organized in collaboration with L2BEAT and Tally) we polled >65 DAO and governance leaders (including many from ArbitrumDAO and the AF) to identify the biggest challenges facing DAOs. 
          Biggest Challenges in DAO Governance  1920×1078 91.8 KB         
 Contributor engagement emerged as one of the most pressing issues, second only to decentralization and effective governance structures. While the results of this exercise may be anecdotal, they highlight the critical importance of onboarding and attracting new talent for DAOs, Arbitrum inclusive. To date, no other viable solutions addressing this challenge have been proposed to the DAO. Instead, other efforts / groups have emerged to either take on or establish structures (e.g., OpCo) for internalizing critical functions, arguably narrowing pathways for external contributors. 
 Given this context,  @RikaGoldberg ’s proposal stands out as the only significant effort directly tackling this issue. We acknowledge that the costs could have been optimized further or the number of fellows increased, but in our view, the proposal’s potential benefits outweigh its shortcomings. For these reasons, we maintain our original position and will vote  FOR  the onchain proposal. 
 
   Proposal:  Tally | Arbitrum | [CONSTITUTIONAL] - Adopt Timeboost + Nova Fee Sweep  
  Vote: FOR  
 Type: On-chain  
 Rationale : We voted in support of this proposal during the snapshot vote and expressed our rationale  here . Our position has remained unchanged and we will be voting FOR the onchain proposal. 
 
   Proposal:  [CONSTITUTIONAL] Proposal: For Arbitrum DAO to register the Sky Custom Gateway contracts in the Router  
  Vote: FOR  
 Type: Off-chain  
 Rationale : This proposal essentially seeks to enable bridging of USDS and sUSDS tokens through the official Arbitrum bridge UI. We are in support of this proposal because it seeks to create a seamless experience for Arbitrum users seeking to access Spark/Sky products, while bearing no costs or risks to the DAO. We’re happy to see stronger alignment between both ecosystems who are leaders in their respective verticals. 
 
   Proposal:  [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Audit Program  
  Vote: ABSTAIN  
 Type: On-chain  
 Rationale : We are fully in support of this initiative by the foundation. Having laid the groundwork for this initiative through our work on the Arbitrum Security Subsidy Fund (SSF) as ADPC members, its exciting to see it being made a core pillar of Arbitrum’s builder support strategy. We believe that security is a critical need for any crypto ecosystem and subsidizing audits is an effective way to incentivize builder participation for Arbitrum. The  results of the SSF  are a testament to this. 
 Although we fully support this proposal, we will be voting ABSTAIN in line with our customary practice of voting neutrally on proposals or initiatives where we’re directly involved.     TMC’s Proposed Allocations  
 I will be voting  YES  on  both  options or mainly supporting the   #1  Deploy Both Strategies option on Snapshot .  The rest of my RCV will be:   #3  ,  2# ,  Abstain ,   #4  , in that order.  
 First, my reasoning for supporting the ARB strategy is that selling pressure FUD isa bit overblown given what the market already dumps on its own naturally. Also, while finding proper insurance is a tough ask, I think the TMC’s numbers have been laid out sufficiently well for us to back this option to alleviate current downside. 
 Regarding the stablecoin strategy, I believe the same applies here. The TMC is not proposing anyting out of this world that the most finance saavy here don’t do already to some degree. Optimizing and testing out these strategies is key so that we can deploy them quicker moving forward. Yes, there’s a inherent risk to both, but we’ll never be able to fully cancel those risks out and we also have to consider the opportunity cost for not implementing either proposal. 
    [CONSTITUTIONAL] - Adopt Timeboost + Nova Fee Sweep  
 Once again, I am voting  FOR  on  Tally . Sentiment is the  same as when I voted on Snapshot , this a no brainer that generally improves the network and adds extra treasury funds. Not much else to add. 
    [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Onboarding V2: A Governance Bootcamp  
 I will be voting  FOR  on  Tally . My reasoning being the following: I became active in the forum around the time this was first pitched and have joined the DIP since then. Creating proper resources for those who want to somehow contribute or work on the DAO or Arbitrum as a whole would help a ton of people through this process, as opposed to having to figure everything out themselves. 
 Building an evergreen curriculum and reusable materials can help anyone get started way quicker, even outside the bootcamp.

{"score":"0.00","tn":5,"rn":0,"breakdown":{"0x9d13c56f911796a578812959f775092b3644901be17738a2d41c516d8a8f2e9b":{"validRationale":false,"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/27208/28","voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/tmc-proposed-allocations-v2-arb-strategy/28785","createdAt":"2025-03-31T09:27:16.000Z","rationale":"Date Voted:  March 31, 2025 
  66. Proposal:   TMC Stablecoin Recommendation  (Snapshot) 
 
 
  Vote:  YES, Deploy Stablecoin Strategy 
 
 
  Rationale:  Just want to add a little since most of my thoughts have already been covered  TMC’s Proposed Allocations - #43 by Ignas  
 Again, this proposal will bring a more sustainable way to manage Arbitrum’s treasury. In a volatile market where not just ARB but most assets can swing hard, moving some funds into stablecoins helps protect the DAO’s value. 
 The expected 8-12% (from Karpatkey and others)  annual return could fund other initiatives to grow the ecosystem. If this works well, the DAO could scale this strategy with a larger amount in the future. 
 
 
  67. Proposal:   TMC ARB Recommendation  (Snapshot) 
 
 
  Vote:  NO, Deploy Nothing 
 
 
  Rationale:  While the proposal introduces potential yield strategies for the treasury but after carefully reviewing the two service providers and the current market conditions, I’m voting NO, Deploy Nothing this proposal. 
 Well, covered calls could generate up to 30% returns, but that heavily depends on market liquidity and price volatility, which nothing is guaranteed. Locking up 10M ARB in this strategy feels too risky at this stage. 
 I believe it’s too soon to commit such a large amount. A more cautious approach with further analysis would be wiser. Hopefully 3 months is enough to get a read on it. 
 
 
  68. Proposal:   OpCo – Oversight and Transparency Committee (OAT) Elections  (Snapshot) 
 
  Vote:  100% MarcZeller 
  Rationale:  Voted for Marc Zeller for the OpCo - Oversight and Transparency Committee. He knows governance, treasury, and strategy inside out, making him a huge asset to Arbitrum. Plus, his vision for efficiency and transparency is exactly what OpCo needs. Confident he’ll do a great job!"},"0xdd38e103e337b9e02a9d9ac73c2587db5be6cfb46506e13c0e897f7331895cf4":{"validRationale":false,"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/27208/28","voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/tmc-proposed-allocations-v2-stablecoin-strategy/28786","createdAt":"2025-03-31T09:26:59.000Z","rationale":"Date Voted:  March 31, 2025 
  66. Proposal:   TMC Stablecoin Recommendation  (Snapshot) 
 
 
  Vote:  YES, Deploy Stablecoin Strategy 
 
 
  Rationale:  Just want to add a little since most of my thoughts have already been covered  TMC’s Proposed Allocations - #43 by Ignas  
 Again, this proposal will bring a more sustainable way to manage Arbitrum’s treasury. In a volatile market where not just ARB but most assets can swing hard, moving some funds into stablecoins helps protect the DAO’s value. 
 The expected 8-12% (from Karpatkey and others)  annual return could fund other initiatives to grow the ecosystem. If this works well, the DAO could scale this strategy with a larger amount in the future. 
 
 
  67. Proposal:   TMC ARB Recommendation  (Snapshot) 
 
 
  Vote:  NO, Deploy Nothing 
 
 
  Rationale:  While the proposal introduces potential yield strategies for the treasury but after carefully reviewing the two service providers and the current market conditions, I’m voting NO, Deploy Nothing this proposal. 
 Well, covered calls could generate up to 30% returns, but that heavily depends on market liquidity and price volatility, which nothing is guaranteed. Locking up 10M ARB in this strategy feels too risky at this stage. 
 I believe it’s too soon to commit such a large amount. A more cautious approach with further analysis would be wiser. Hopefully 3 months is enough to get a read on it. 
 
 
  68. Proposal:   OpCo – Oversight and Transparency Committee (OAT) Elections  (Snapshot) 
 
  Vote:  100% MarcZeller

  66. Proposal:   TMC Stablecoin Recommendation  (Snapshot)

  Rationale:  Just want to add a little since most of my thoughts have already been covered  TMC’s Proposed Allocations - #43 by Ignas  
 Again, this proposal will bring a more sustainable way to manage Arbitrum’s treasury. In a volatile market where not just ARB but most assets can swing hard, moving some funds into stablecoins helps protect the DAO’s value. 
 The expected 8-12% (from Karpatkey and others)  annual return could fund other initiatives to grow the ecosystem. If this works well, the DAO could scale this strategy with a larger amount in the future. 
 
 
  67. Proposal:   TMC ARB Recommendation  (Snapshot) 
 
 
  Vote:  NO, Deploy Nothing 
 
 
  Rationale:  While the proposal introduces potential yield strategies for the treasury but after carefully reviewing the two service providers and the current market conditions, I’m voting NO, Deploy Nothing this proposal. 
 Well, covered calls could generate up to 30% returns, but that heavily depends on market liquidity and price volatility, which nothing is guaranteed. Locking up 10M ARB in this strategy feels too risky at this stage. 
 I believe it’s too soon to commit such a large amount. A more cautious approach with further analysis would be wiser. Hopefully 3 months is enough to get a read on it. 
 
 
  68. Proposal:   OpCo – Oversight and Transparency Committee (OAT) Elections  (Snapshot)

  Voting type & platform : Offchain - Snapshot 
  Vote :
 
 Only deploy stable strategy 
 Deploy nothing 
 Abstain 
 Deploy both strategies 
 Only Deploy ARB Strategy 
 
 
  Reasoning : 
In line with the trust we have placed in TMC in the recent past, as stated in our Treasury Management Rationale ( Snapshot  &  Tally ), we have aligned ourselves with their suggestions and our first choice was to “deploy only the stable strategy”, leaving no or little room for the other choices. We appreciate this recommendation as we can clearly see that they are acting in accordance with the tasks they have been entrusted with, which is mainly to protect the DAO treasury. Moreover, as the proposal itself indicates, the TMC remains open to future ARB allocation strategies that are less risky, so it is not a definitive no to this idea. When circumstances improve, especially with respect to the current market conditions that have affected the price of ARB, that will be the right time to move forward with this strategy. We see it as a prudent move not to do this at this moment and only to implement the stablecoin allocation strategy, which at the same time has been evenly split between 3 well trusted selected partners with good reputations both inside and outside the DAO, and even though 2 of them are also part of the part that we are rejecting of this proposal, when it comes to the stablecoin allocation strategies presented we see that the potential benefits outweigh our concerns, unlike what happens with the ARB allocation part of their projects."},"0xdd38e103e337b9e02a9d9ac73c2587db5be6cfb46506e13c0e897f7331895cf4":{"validRationale":false,"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/24722/95","voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/tmc-proposed-allocations-v2-stablecoin-strategy/28786","createdAt":"2025-04-02T13:01:47.000Z","rationale":"Proposal :   TMC Recommendation

Metagovernance Results: FOR  
 Below is a consolidated summary of the rationales for the Arbitrum Growth Circle proposal. 
  – Rationale 1 – Community Empowerment & Sustainable Ecosystem Growth:  
A large majority of supporters (78 votes) argue that the proposal’s primary strength is its focus on building a scalable, peer‑led support network that empowers early‑stage protocols and fosters community-led innovation. They emphasize that by investing in knowledge sharing, mentorship, and network-building, the initiative will promote long‑term sustainability, transparent governance, and indirect benefits such as enhanced token utility and liquidity. These voters see the Growth Circle as a way to strengthen the ecosystem over time, indirectly supporting token value and broader adoption. 
  – Opposition Due to Misalignment with Immediate Token Value/Revenue Priorities:  
A smaller group (12 votes) opposed the proposal because they feel its focus on community building and long‑term ecosystem development does not directly address their core priorities—namely, near-term tokenomics, revenue‑sharing, staking initiatives, or specific technical improvements. These voters are more concerned with initiatives that deliver direct, high‑yield returns or targeted technical upgrades and therefore view the Growth Circle as not sufficiently aligned with their aggressive or revenue‑focused investment strategies. 
  ehARB-83 Request to Increase the Stylus Sprint Committee’s Budget 
Metagovernance Results: FOR  
 Below is a consolidated summary of the rationales provided for the Stylus Sprint Grant Program proposal, grouped by similar themes: 
  – Support for Arbitrum and Stylus Ecosystem Growth  
Several voters emphasize that the proposal supports crucial ecosystem growth for both Arbitrum and Stylus, contributing to their long‑term success and further development.  Count:  3 votes 
  – Funding Promising Unfunded Projects  
Some voters highlight the importance of this program in funding high‑potential projects that were previously unfunded, thus helping to accelerate innovation and new ideas.  Count:  3 votes 
  – Facilitating Innovative and Impactful Ideas  
A few voters note that the program helps facilitate new, innovative ideas and has a positive impact on the broader ecosystem.  Count:  2 votes 
  – Opposition – Need for New Grant Season and Application Period  
One voter opposes extending the previous series of grants without reopening the application period, arguing that it should be treated as a new season.  Count:  1 vote 
In total, these themes reflect the views of 8 voters, with some voters mentioning more than one rationale in their response. 
  ehARB-82 Arbitrum D.A.O. (Domain Allocator Offerings) Grant Program 
Metagovernance Results: FOR  
 Below is a consolidated summary of the rationales for the Domain Allocator/Arbitrum D.A.O. Grant proposal, with similar themes grouped and the number of votes reflecting how many voters expressed that rationale (noting that some voters mention multiple points): 
  – Funding Promising Projects & Supporting Ecosystem Growth  
Several voters emphasize that the proposal will fund projects that have been overlooked in the past and help drive growth in both the Arbitrum and Stylus ecosystems. Count: 3 votes 
  – Facilitating Innovative and Impactful Ideas  
Some voters note that the proposal will enable innovative, high‑impact projects and ideas that can drive broader ecosystem progress. Count: 2 votes. 
  – Trust in the Program’s Success and Budget  
Several voters mentioned a history of success previously seen including: “The Domain Allocator Program has been essential on funding great initiatives on previous seasons and I’m confident they will keep delivering value.” And, This is an important grant program to serve asks under 50k. The domains are well thought and it is the continuation of a successful program. 
  – Misalignment with Immediate Tokenomics & Revenue Priorities (Against)  
Several opponents (8 votes) contend that while community building is valuable in the long run, the proposal does not directly address their primary interests in immediate token value gains, revenue sharing, or staking mechanisms. They feel that the initiative’s focus on broad ecosystem growth is too indirect to meet their short‑term, high‑yield objectives. 
  – Process Concerns – Need for a Fresh Application Period  
One voter objects to simply extending the previous grant program without reopening the application process, arguing instead for a new season with a fresh application period 
  – Concerns Over Methodology & Strategic Fit (Against)  
A smaller group (roughly 5) criticizes the underlying methodology—citing past performance reports and a predisposition to oppose proposals—as well as the overall strategic fit. Including: “The Domain Allocator methodology in this proposal is not a good one for a grants program this long as it was demonstrated by the Entropy report on last season projects.” 
  Final Overview:  Out of 96 total votes, 79 support the proposal while 17 oppose it. The supporters almost universally see the proposal as a means to empower the community, enhance transparent governance, and build a sustainable support network that will ultimately boost token utility and value. In contrast, opponents focus on its lack of direct, immediate mechanisms for revenue sharing or token value improvement and raise concerns about its methodology or strategic fit for high‑yield, aggressive investment approaches. Forced votes—issued per external instruction—account for a handful of the opposition tallies. 
  ehARB-81 Approve the Nova Fee Sweep Action 
Metagovernance Results: FOR  
 Summary of Votes and Rationales for Fee Collection Proposal 
  – Unanimous Support –  There are no opposing votes, indicating broad consensus that this proposal brings efficiency and financial benefits to the DAO. 
  – Simplifying the Fee Collection Process  
Multiple voters support the proposal because it streamlines and improves the efficiency of collecting fees. 
  – Supporting the DAO Treasury  
Voters see this proposal as a way to ensure more funds are directed toward the DAO Treasury, strengthening its financial sustainability. 
  – Aligning with Updated Infrastructure  
At least one voter supports the proposal because it aligns with improvements in the DAO’s operational framework. 
  ehARB-80 Non-Constitutional: Stable Treasury Endowment Program 2.0 
Metagovernance Results: AGAINST  
 Below is a consolidated summary of the rationales provided for the treasury diversification proposal, with similar themes grouped and counts indicating how many votes mention each theme: 
  – Rationale 1 – Treasury Diversification for Stability  
Voters emphasize that diversifying the treasury is key to enhancing overall financial stability. Count: 4 votes 
  – Rationale 2: Belief in the Growth of the RWA Ecosystem  
Some voters note that a diversified treasury can support and benefit the growing real‑world assets (RWA) ecosystem.Count: 2 votes 
  – Rationale 3: Potential for Generating Consistent Yields  
Several voters point out that the program is expected to generate consistent yields, which would contribute to both stability and growth. Count: 3 votes 
  – Rationale 4: Opposition – Preference for Existing Treasury Management Structures  
One voter argues that treasury diversification should be managed under the existing Treasury Manager frameworks (TMC/GMC) instead of creating a new structure. Including: “This should be handled by Treasury Managers under TMC or GMC. It does not make sense to pay for 2 different control/management structures.” Count: 1 vote 
  – Rational 5: Opposition – Preference for an Alternative Use of Funds  
One voter prefers allocating the funds elsewhere, indicating a different strategic priority. Count: 1 vote 
 In total, these themes capture the views of 9 voters, with some voters mentioning more than one rationale in their responses. 
  ehARB-79 OpCo: A DAO-adjacent Entity for Strategy Execution 
Metagovernance Results: AGAINST  
 Summary of Votes and Rationales for the OpCo Proposal: 
  – Streamlining DAO Operations (Efficiency & Management)  
Several voters believe that OpCo will help manage DAO operations more effectively, reducing delays and improving execution. 
  – Enhancing Contributor Effectiveness  
Some voters see OpCo as a way to empower contributors and improve governance participation. 
  – Managing Complex Initiatives Better  
Some voters believe OpCo will help handle large-scale, complex DAO initiatives more efficiently. 
  – Addressing DAO Delays & Bottlenecks  
One voter supports OpCo as a way to solve Arbitrum DAO’s inefficiencies and reduce delays. Including: “OpCo aims to help the draw backs of Arb dao, the delays it took while doing works, it would like to take things on hand and make it move faster” 
  – Concerns About Centralization & Gatekeeping  
Multiple voters are concerned that OpCo could lead to excessive centralization, restricting contributor access and control. Including: “I’m worried that OpCo might lead to centralization or gatekeeping for further contributors. It doesn’t follow the ethos that I support.” And, “This will centralize operations of Arbitrum, if we truly believe on decentralization then this is something we should avoid.” 
  – Too Much Funding at Once  
One voter believes the proposal requests too much funding upfront and suggests testing smaller initiatives first. Including: “this is too much money, all at once. We should just fund the OAT and see how that performs for example, and not the whole endeavor at once.” 
 Overall, supporters see efficiency, better management, and faster execution, while critics worry about centralization and excessive funding allocation.","penalty":0}}}

Date: 23/1/25 
 Link  
Vote: Against 
Rationale: The budget has reduced significantly following the previous proposals which is positive, however, we have concerns about spend and the utility of having this hub. It feels like something that could be done cheaper and does not add significant value above the Arbitrum Foundation’s website that provides similar information. 
  Arbitrum Strategic Objective Setting (SOS) – Defining the DAO’s Interim Goals  
Date: 24/1/25 
 Link  
Vote: For 
Rationale: We support this as a next logical step towards implementing the mission, vision, and purpose of the DAO. 
  Non-Constitutional: Stable Treasury Endowment Program 2.0  
Date: 31/1/25 
 Link  
Vote: For 
Rationale: We believe this is a good step forward for treasury management within the DAO. It both earns yield and supports new sectors within DeFi on Arbitrum, both worthy goals. 
  OpCo: A DAO-adjacent Entity for Strategy Execution  
Date: 31/1/25 
 Link  
Vote: Against 
Rationale: We voted AGAINST as we do not believe this level of spend is necessary to achieve the objectives. We believe that spending by Arbitrum DAO has become excessive and further initiatives involving large spend should be frozen until a cleanup has taken place. 
  Approve the Nova Fee Sweep Action  
Date: 31/1/25 
 Link  
Vote: For 
Rationale: We believe this is beneficial to the DAO with no drawbacks, simply fixing an artefact of the old, now deprecated infrastructure. No reason to vote against this in our opinion. 
  Request to Increase the Stylus Sprint Committee’s Budget  
Date: 7/2/25 
 Link  
Vote: Against 
Rationale: We do not believe this is a sensible use of DAO funds. This nearly doubles the initial budget and we feel this is a clear case of budget creep. It suggests that either the committee administering this program lacks discipline, or the criteria used to score projects are deficient. This proposal also violates the soft funding cap laid out in the initial budget proposal. If additional budget is needed, the impact of initial funding should be assessed along with the criteria used for scoring, followed by another funding round. 
  Arbitrum D.A.O. (Domain Allocator Offerings) Grant Program - Season 3  
Date: 7/2/25 
 Link  
Vote: Against 
Rationale: We previously voted FOR the snapshot of this, but having further considered the state of the DAO and spending, we believe a freeze of all non-essential spending is needed until a cleanup has taken place. We have therefore voted AGAINST this proposal on Tally."},"# [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Audit Pr":{"validRationale":false,"voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":null,"createdAt":"2025-03-24T09:34:17.000Z","post":null,"reason":null,"rationale":"Arbitrum Growth Circles Event Proposal

 Link  
Vote: Against 
Rationale: This proposal is for an events program. We do not believe that spend on events programs at this time is appropriate given the low ROI and various conversations taking place in the DAO around reducing unnecessary and bloated spending. 
  [CONSTITUTIONAL] AIP: ArbOS Version 40 Callisto  
Date: 21/2/25 
 Link  
Vote: For 
Rationale: We support this technical upgrade and believe it is a positive step forward for Arbitrum. 
  Arbitrum Audit Program  
Date: 21/2/25 
 Link  
Vote: For 
Rationale: $10m budget is not a cheap program but due to the support from OCL and the Foundation we believe this will have a high chance of success. We can see the merit to supporting builders with audit costs as it is a significant financial barrier to secure, high quality launches by smaller independent teams. 
  Request to Increase the Stylus Sprint Committee’s Budget  
Date: 21/2/25 
 Link  
Vote: Against 
Rationale: We do not believe this is a sensible use of DAO funds. This nearly doubles the initial budget and we feel this is a clear case of budget creep. It suggests that either: the committee administering this program lacks discipline, or the criteria used to score projects are deficient. We also believe this proposal violates the soft funding cap laid out in the initial budget proposal. Either way, if this program were to need additional budget the impact of initial funding should be assessed along with the criteria used for scoring and then another funding round be carried out.","penalty":0}}}

{"score":"0.00","tn":5,"rn":0,"breakdown":{"0x9d13c56f911796a578812959f775092b3644901be17738a2d41c516d8a8f2e9b":{"validRationale":null,"post":null,"voted":false,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/tmc-proposed-allocations-v2-arb-strategy/28785","createdAt":null},"0xdd38e103e337b9e02a9d9ac73c2587db5be6cfb46506e13c0e897f7331895cf4":{"validRationale":null,"post":null,"voted":false,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/tmc-proposed-allocations-v2-stablecoin-strategy/28786","createdAt":null},"0xb422848b0f53f9077444b922d553618e331f9abe7539ae4566ff84364d0fbd3c":{"validRationale":null,"post":null,"voted":false,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/opco-oversight-and-transparency-committee-oat-elections/28867","createdAt":null},"0x7689f957c8cc37e991c7bed0f1d3dd3f6024a921b0bfbae5c897a09ab45c538b":{"validRationale":false,"post":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/21561/20","voted":false,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":"https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/non-constitutional-rfc-arb-incentives-user-acquisition-for-dapps-protocols/","createdAt":null,"rationale":"February 2025 
 ////////////////////////// 
   Snapshot   
 [1]  Approve the Nova Fee Sweep Action  
 Vote:  For 
 Rationale:  
This proposal finalises a more efficient and transparent fee collection system for Nova. Transferring the accumulated fees from the legacy L1TimelockAlias contract into the DAO treasury ensures that these resources can be put to better use in support of the Arbitrum ecosystem. 
 [2]  Arbitrum Growth Circles Event Proposal  
 Vote:  For 
 Rationale:  
We have decided to support this proposal, primarily because we believe it offers a constructive pathway to foster growth for protocols in the Arbitrum ecosystem. While concerns have been raised about the budget, we feel that the overall potential for bolstering community-led engagement and knowledge-sharing is a worthy investment. 
 We also note that the same team behind the Arbitrum Ventures Initiative (AVI) is proposing this event series, which suggests they already have a suitable audience of protocols in need of these support sessions. Given that the Arbitrum Ventures Initiative (AVI) was established to direct strategic ecosystem investments and expand Arbitrum’s reach among emerging protocols, it has already built a robust network of founders and industry partners. 
 However, we do share the community’s reservations regarding the softness of the proposed KPIs. Even though we are voting in favour, we would like to see more robust and quantifiable metrics in the final implementation. A clearer definition of success—such as the number of protocols effectively onboarded, specific liquidity or usage milestones achieved, or longer-term retention—would help ensure accountability and transparency in measuring this initiative’s true value. 
 [3]  [CONSTITUTIONAL] AIP: ArbOS Version 40 Callisto  
 Vote:  For 
 Rationale:  
We support this constitutional AIP because upgrading Arbitrum One and Nova to the latest ArbOS version is essential for keeping both networks current, secure, and high-performing. We see no compelling reason to vote against a necessary software update that aligns with the broader goal of maintaining Arbitrum’s technical edge. 
 [4]  Arbitrum Audit Program  
 Vote:  For 
 Rationale:  
We believe this proposal addresses a key challenge for small to medium protocols that cannot afford the high cost of audits. By lowering barriers to thorough security checks, the initiative will help these projects safely grow within the Arbitrum ecosystem. While we acknowledge there may be misalignment on who precisely should execute and oversee the programme, we view audits as indispensable for maintaining ecosystem trust. 
 Accordingly, we suggest forming a joint committee—comprising representatives from the ADPC and the Arbitrum Foundation—to manage the programme. This dual approach would align resources and expertise. 
 ////////////////////////// 
   Tally   
 [1]  Non-Constitutional: Stable Treasury Endowment Program 2.0  
 Vote:  For 
 Rationale:  
We already support this proposal in the Sanpshot phase thus we will in the Tally phase. We support STEP 2.0 as it strengthens RWA as a key narrative for Arbitrum, positioning the ecosystem at the forefront of real-world asset adoption. The initial implementation of STEP demonstrated strong execution and stability, and we believe expanding this initiative builds on that solid foundation. Diversifying treasury assets is essential for long-term sustainability, mitigating exposure to crypto market volatility while generating stable cash flow. 
 [2]  Arbitrum D.A.O. (Domain Allocator Offerings) Grant Program - Season 3  
 Vote:  For 
 Rationale:  
We already vote for the Domain Allocator applicants in the Snapshot phase. We will supported the winning participant in the Tally phase. 
  Forwarding our rationales from the Snapshot phase:  
We have voted to renew the grant program for Season 3, expanding to five domains by adding Orbit. In previous seasons, the program demonstrated acceptable results; for instance, Season 1 achieved a completion rate of 73%. Therefore, we believe it is prudent to continue into Season 3. 
 Orbit is essential to the Arbitrum ecosystem as it enables developers to launch customizable Layer 2 and Layer 3 chains, enhancing scalability and interoperability within the network. Consequently, we support the inclusion of five domains in the upcoming season."},"# [NON-CONSTITUTIONAL] Arbitrum Audit Pr":{"validRationale":false,"voted":true,"updated":"automatically","proposalTopic":null,"createdAt":"2025-03-27T14:04:40.000Z","post":null,"reason":null,"rationale":"February 2025

We have decided to support this proposal, primarily because we believe it offers a constructive pathway to foster growth for protocols in the Arbitrum ecosystem. While concerns have been raised about the budget, we feel that the overall potential for bolstering community-led engagement and knowledge-sharing is a worthy investment. 
 We also note that the same team behind the Arbitrum Ventures Initiative (AVI) is proposing this event series, which suggests they already have a suitable audience of protocols in need of these support sessions. Given that the Arbitrum Ventures Initiative (AVI) was established to direct strategic ecosystem investments and expand Arbitrum’s reach among emerging protocols, it has already built a robust network of founders and industry partners.

Even though we are voting in favour, we would like to see more robust and quantifiable metrics in the final implementation. A clearer definition of success—such as the number of protocols effectively onboarded, specific liquidity or usage milestones achieved, or longer-term retention—would help ensure accountability and transparency in measuring this initiative’s true value. 
 [3]  [CONSTITUTIONAL] AIP: ArbOS Version 40 Callisto  
 Vote:  For 
 Rationale:  
We support this constitutional AIP because upgrading Arbitrum One and Nova to the latest ArbOS version is essential for keeping both networks current, secure, and high-performing. We see no compelling reason to vote against a necessary software update that aligns with the broader goal of maintaining Arbitrum’s technical edge. 
 [4]  Arbitrum Audit Program  
 Vote:  For 
 Rationale:  
We believe this proposal addresses a key challenge for small to medium protocols that cannot afford the high cost of audits. By lowering barriers to thorough security checks, the initiative will help these projects safely grow within the Arbitrum ecosystem. While we acknowledge there may be misalignment on who precisely should execute and oversee the programme, we view audits as indispensable for maintaining ecosystem trust. 
 Accordingly, we suggest forming a joint committee—comprising representatives from the ADPC and the Arbitrum Foundation—to manage the programme. This dual approach would align resources and expertise. 
 ////////////////////////// 
   Tally   
 [1]  Non-Constitutional: Stable Treasury Endowment Program 2.0  
 Vote:  For 
 Rationale:  
We already support this proposal in the Sanpshot phase thus we will in the Tally phase. We support STEP 2.0 as it strengthens RWA as a key narrative for Arbitrum, positioning the ecosystem at the forefront of real-world asset adoption. The initial implementation of STEP demonstrated strong execution and stability, and we believe expanding this initiative builds on that solid foundation. Diversifying treasury assets is essential for long-term sustainability, mitigating exposure to crypto market volatility while generating stable cash flow. 
 [2]  Arbitrum D.A.O. (Domain Allocator Offerings) Grant Program - Season 3  
 Vote:  For 
 Rationale:  
We already vote for the Domain Allocator applicants in the Snapshot phase. We will supported the winning participant in the Tally phase. 
  Forwarding our rationales from the Snapshot phase:  
We have voted to renew the grant program for Season 3, expanding to five domains by adding Orbit. In previous seasons, the program demonstrated acceptable results; for instance, Season 1 achieved a completion rate of 73%. Therefore, we believe it is prudent to continue into Season 3. 
 Orbit is essential to the Arbitrum ecosystem as it enables developers to launch customizable Layer 2 and Layer 3 chains, enhancing scalability and interoperability within the network. Consequently, we support the inclusion of five domains in the upcoming season.","penalty":0}}}
